The club

Football, taken
seriously

Royal Eagles FC runs competitive football for players who want to be coached properly. We field a men's side, a women's side, and youth groups — same crest, same standards, same pitch.

What started as one squad now covers multiple teams and a growing academy. Our players train in structured sessions, play real fixtures, and represent a club that turns up.

Qualified coachingStructured curriculum, not kickabouts.
Women's footballA full women's squad, competing and visible.
Youth pathwayAcademy groups feeding the senior sides.
The squads

Who wears the crest

Three sides, one club. Every player registered, kitted, and playing competitive fixtures.

Men's First TeamCompetitive fixtures
Women's TeamFull competitive squad
The Full SquadRoyal Eagles Football Club & Academy
Partners

Backed by
institutions

National Bank of Pakistan put their name on our shirt.

Sponsorship isn't decoration — it's a signal that the club is run properly enough for a national institution to attach its brand to it. Our kit carries the NBP mark across every squad.

We're now opening conversations with partners who want visibility across men's, women's and youth football, in Pakistan and in the UAE.

Discuss sponsorship
The academy

Where players are made

Structured after-school and weekend coaching across age groups — technical work, tactical understanding, and real match play.

Junior Groups Ball mastery, coordination and first touch. Small-sided games every session so players actually play. U8 – U12
Youth Development Position-specific work, tactical shape and physical conditioning as players move toward competitive football. U14 – U16
Senior Pathway The bridge into our competitive squads — match fitness, set pieces, and selection for real fixtures. U18 & Senior
Partnership

Why back Royal Eagles

We're expanding the academy model into the UAE, building on an established club with squads, sponsorship and a track record already in place.

Proven on the pitchMultiple squads already fielded, kitted and competing — not a concept looking for a first team.
Institutional trustAn existing national bank sponsorship demonstrates the club clears real due diligence.
Room to growAcademy programs, school partnerships and facility investment across Pakistan and the UAE.
